prompt:
Take the provided reference image and reproduce it PIXEL-FAITHFULLY — same overall composition (upper hand cradling the device, lower hand pulling the vial down to detach it, vertical down-arrow indicating detachment direction), same device appearance (balloon-shape, flush red button with center black dot, lower neck protrusion), same vial filled halfway with blood, same line-art style on pure white background, same line weight. DO NOT change the hands, the composition, the device, the red button, the blood fill, the arrow, the line weight, or the style.

TWO MODIFICATIONS — restore the asymmetric stepped connector geometry that should appear when vial-meets-device:

1) VIAL'S OPEN STEPPED RIM at its TOP (the end pulled out of the device): the open mouth of the vial is STRONGLY ASYMMETRIC — one half of the rim is raised as a SHORT BLUNT ANGULAR tongue (flush with the side wall, height ≈ half its width, peak blunt and crisp-edged, NOT a smooth dome). The OTHER half of the rim is a flat shorter lip. A clean visible vertical step between the two halves in the middle of the rim. The raised tongue is on (say) the FRONT-LEFT side of the rim (visible in 3/4 view).

2) DEVICE'S CONNECTOR PORT END (the bottom of the device's neck protrusion, where the vial was just pulled out): show the matching COMPLEMENTARY stepped shape. The device's port end is ALSO asymmetric — one half of its bottom edge extends DOWN as a SHORT BLUNT ANGULAR tongue (same shape language as the vial's tongue, flush with the neck's side wall, height ≈ half its width). The OTHER half is a flat shorter lip. CRITICAL: the device's raised tongue is on the OPPOSITE side from the vial's raised tongue (so when the vial was inserted, the two stepped ends interlocked as mirror-complementary jaws). If the vial's raised tongue is on the FRONT-LEFT, the device's raised tongue is on the BACK-RIGHT (and vice versa).

Both stepped ends are now visible because the vial has been pulled away from the device, exposing them. The geometry tells the viewer that the vial and device fit together like puzzle pieces.

Do not modify anything else — no changes to the hands, the device's overall shape (still balloon with neck protrusion), the red button, the blood, the line weight, the background, or the style. Pure black line-art on white with TWO REDS (flush red button + red blood). No frame, no border around the canvas, no labels.
